---
pagetitle: "Philippine Departmental Budget Reviews"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    theme: null
    mathjax: null
    highlight: null
---

<!--
============================================================================
  HOME / INDEX PAGE  ->  knit this file to produce  index.html
============================================================================
  To update the landing page you only edit the chunk directly below:

    * site_title / site_subtitle / site_intro / source_note  -> page text
    * the `agencies` table                                   -> one row per agency

  For each agency row:
    short      short label shown in the badge (e.g. "DepEd")
    name       full department name
    unit       sub-unit line (e.g. "Office of the Secretary"); "" for none
    output     the knitted report's HTML filename in docs/ (the link target)
    status     "published"  -> clickable card
               "planned"    -> greyed-out "coming soon" card
    blurb      one-sentence description (used only when published)
    highlights up to a few short stats, separated by "  |  "
               (used only when published; leave "" otherwise)

  After editing, knit to index.html and place it in docs/ alongside the
  report HTML files. No data file or other scripts are required.
============================================================================
-->

```{r config, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

# ---- EDIT THIS: page text ------------------------------------------------
site_title    <- "Philippine Departmental Budget Reviews"
site_subtitle <- "Budget evolution and absorptive capacity, FY 2018-2026"
site_intro    <- paste(
  "These reviews go beyond the headline figures. For each department we",
  "compile the proposed (NEP) and enacted (GAA) budgets down to the level of",
  "individual Programs, Activities, and Projects (P/A/Ps), then match them",
  "against the agency Financial Accountability Reports (the SAAODB) that",
  "record how each peso was actually released, obligated, and disbursed.",
  "Assembling these sources at the P/A/P level is painstaking, but it is what",
  "makes a sharper reading possible \u2014 not just how much a department",
  "received, but where Congress redirected it, which programs could absorb",
  "their funding, and where promised spending never materialized. Every",
  "report pairs the analysis with sortable, exportable reference tables so",
  "you can trace the figures yourself; coverage and scope vary by agency, as",
  "each report's data note explains."
)
source_note <- paste(
  "Underlying data: DBM-published budget and execution data (Current New",
  "Appropriations only). NEP/GAA cover FY 2018-2026; execution measures",
  "(Allotments, Obligations, Disbursements) cover FY 2018-2025. Coverage",
  "varies by agency; the DPWH review instead traces appropriations to 1988."
)
repo_url <- "https://github.com/ajamontesa/ph-budget-analysis/"   # GitHub repo: full code + datasets

# ---- EDIT THIS: one row per agency ---------------------------------------
agencies <- tibble::tribble(
  ~short,   ~name,                                                ~unit,                     ~output,            ~status,      ~blurb, ~highlights,

  "DAR",    "Department of Agrarian Reform",                      "Office of the Secretary", "dar-osec.html",    "published",
  "A small department, and one Congress tends to trim rather than augment. A flat \u20b18\u201310B budget for eight years jumps 62% in 2026 \u2014 built almost entirely on foreign-assisted loan projects that are the agency's weakest absorbers.",
  "Flat \u20b18\u201310B for 8 years \u2192 \u20b116.8B in 2026 (+62%)  |  Congress is a net subtractor: \u2212\u20b12.4B (2022), \u2212\u20b14.5B (2023)  |  Foreign-assisted projects 10% \u2192 37% of budget, but disburse just 19\u201338%",

  "DA",     "Department of Agriculture",                          "OSEC + 9 attached agencies", "da-osec.html",     "published",
  "A striking case of appropriation outrunning execution. The OSEC budget nearly quadrupled, but disbursement fell to 69% \u2014 with two giant lines (FMR, which DA never executes, and the LFP/FAP black box) crowding out a rice-dominated operational core. Adds deep-dives by service type and by commodity.",
  "OSEC GAA \u20b143.9B (2018) \u2192 \u20b1165.5B (2026), 3.8\u00d7; D/A fell to 69%  |  FMR \u20b133B (zero DA execution) + LFP/FAP \u20b172B = 63% of 2026 OSEC  |  Operational core ~75% PSS, ~63% rice; equipment & livestock absorb worst (33\u201341%)",

  "DepEd",  "Department of Education",                            "Office of the Secretary", "deped-osec.html",  "published",
  "A payroll-dominated budget whose strong headline absorption masks chronic under-spending in the non-salary tail \u2014 textbooks, facilities, and the collapsing GASTPE subsidies.",
  "GAA grew 1.7\u00d7: \u20b1552B (2018) \u2192 \u20b1959B (2026)  |  Record \u20b186B Congressional augmentation in 2026  |  Textbooks disbursed just 2\u201317% of allotment every year",

  "DOE",    "Department of Energy",                               "Office of the Secretary", "doe.html",         "planned", "", "",

  "DENR",   "Department of Environment and Natural Resources",    "Office of the Secretary", "denr-osec.html",   "published",
  "A frozen budget: held flat at \u20b116\u201320B for nine years \u2014 a ~30% real-terms cut \u2014 while Congress trims the proposal and guts the reforestation line, and the department shifts from capital works toward payroll.",
  "Flat ~\u20b119B for 9 years \u2014 \u2248\u20b113.6B in 2018 pesos (~30% real cut)  |  Congress trims; Forest Development cut \u20b16.6B\u2192\u20b12.4B  |  Capital Outlays fell 37%\u219212%; administration is the largest line (21%)",

  "DOH",    "Department of Health",                               "Office of the Secretary", "doh-osec.html",    "published",
  "An aggressively Congress-augmented budget where the binding constraint is a capital-outlay bottleneck \u2014 the infrastructure pipeline (HFEP) cannot disburse what it is given.",
  "GAA nearly tripled: \u20b1107B (2018) \u2192 \u20b1298B (2026)  |  \u20b1210B cumulative plus-ups; MAIFIP + HFEP = 73%  |  Capital Outlays disburse at only 14\u201337% and falling",

  "DOLE",   "Department of Labor and Employment",                 "Whole department + attached agencies", "dole.html",        "published",
  "A large labor department that is, to a first approximation, one program: TUPAD emergency employment is about 70% of it. The budget surged during COVID and stayed high, Congress adds heavily (almost all to TUPAD), and in 2023 three agencies left for the new Department of Migrant Workers. Execution is read at the agency level only.",
  "TUPAD \u2248 \u20b125B \u2014 about 70% of the department (excl. TESDA)  |  COVID surge: \u20b111B (2018) \u2192 \u20b151B (2022) \u2192 ~\u20b130\u201340B since  |  Congress adds +\u20b156B to TUPAD cumulatively; OWWA/POEA/NMP \u2192 DMW in 2023",

  "DSWD",   "Department of Social Welfare and Development",       "Office of the Secretary", "dswd-osec.html",   "published",
  "An almost pure cash-transfer budget \u2014 4Ps, Protective Services, and the Social Pension \u2014 where Congress reshapes the portfolio from within: trimming 4Ps, scaling up Protective Services, and inventing AKAP by appropriation alone.",
  "GAA nearly doubled: \u20b1141B (2018) \u2192 \u20b1264B (2026)  |  Three cash programs = 74\u201388% of the budget; 93\u201395% MOOE  |  Congress redirects: Protective Services +\u20b1116B, 4Ps \u2212\u20b183B",

  "DPWH",   "Department of Public Works and Highways",            "Whole department + operating units", "dpwh.html",        "published",
  "A different angle: with no P/A/P-level execution data, this review traces DPWH's budget back to 1988 and maps how Congress redirects money \u2014 every year \u2014 from the Central Office toward named district engineering offices.",
  "Footprint doubled to a mid-teens budget share; 2026 GAA collapses below 8%  |  2019 line-item veto: \u20b195.4B struck, \u20b174B from the Central Office  |  A four-signal screen flags Batangas 4th, Sorsogon, Abra & Bulacan 1st",

  "TESDA",  "Technical Education and Skills Development Authority","",                        "tesda.html",       "planned", "", "",

  "NCIP",   "National Commission on Indigenous Peoples",          "Whole commission (no P/A/P execution)", "ncip.html",        "published",
  "A very small agency carrying an outsized IPRA mandate. With no FAR No. 1, execution is read only at the agency level \u2014 but the budget story is clear: a near-doubling since 2018, a sharp 2025 cut, and an administration-heavy mix, across two reorganizations.",
  "Budget nearly doubled: \u20b1968M (2018) \u2192 \u20b11,804M (2026), +86%  |  FY2025 cut 25% below proposal, across the board; capital outlay zeroed  |  Admin + policy = 46% of 2026; agency obligation rate 92\u201399%",

  "NCMF",   "National Commission on Muslim Filipinos",            "Whole commission (no P/A/P execution)", "ncmf.html",        "published",
  "A small commission with no FAR No. 1, so execution is read only at the agency level. A fast grower (+145% since 2018), and unusually concentrated: a single cultural-heritage line is about half the budget, and Congress reliably tops it up.",
  "Budget more than doubled: \u20b1535M (2018) \u2192 \u20b11,314M (2026), +145%  |  One line \u2014 Muslim cultural centers & heritage \u2014 is ~46% of 2026; top 3 = 71%  |  Congress tops up 8 of 9 years; agency obligation rate 86\u201399%",

  "PCW",    "Philippine Commission on Women",                     "Whole commission", "pcw.html",         "published",
  "The lead agency on gender mainstreaming, at the million-peso scale \u2014 small enough to read line by line. The budget nearly doubled, Congress tops it up every year (and funds a recurring stream of one-off \u201cTier 2\u201d lines), but absorption is softening as MOOE outpaces what the agency can spend.",
  "GAA nearly doubled: \u20b1112M (2018) \u2192 \u20b1205M (2026), +83%  |  +\u20b129M 2026 add went to one line \u2014 Technical Assistance is now ~a third of the budget  |  Absorption ~80%, softening to 75% (2025); MOOE allotments doubled but disburse 67%"
)

# ---- EDIT THIS: briefing slides --------------------------------------------
# Map an agency's short code to its slide deck under docs/ (i.e. "slides/<file>.html").
# A "Briefing slides" link appears on that row only when an entry is present here,
# so leave a code out until its deck is actually in docs/slides/ (avoids dead links).
# Works for published AND planned agencies (e.g. slides can go up before the report).
# Example, once the files exist:
#   slides_paths <- c(
#     DepEd = "slides/deped-osec.html",
#     DENR  = "slides/denr-osec.html",
#     DA    = "slides/da-osec.html"
#   )
slides_paths <- c(
  DAR   = "slides/DAR_OSEC_Budget_Analysis.html",
  DA    = "slides/DA_Budget_Analysis.html",
  DepEd = "slides/DepEd_OSEC_Budget_Analysis.html",
  DENR  = "slides/DENR_OSEC_Budget_Analysis.html",
  DOH   = "slides/DOH_OSEC_Budget_Analysis.html",
  DSWD  = "slides/DSWD_OSEC_Budget_Analysis.html",
  DOLE  = "slides/DOLE_Budget_Analysis.html",
  NCIP  = "slides/NCIP_Budget_Analysis.html",
  NCMF  = "slides/NCMF_Budget_Analysis.html",
  PCW   = "slides/PCW_Budget_Analysis.html"
)
```

```{css, echo=FALSE}
:root { --ink:#1a1a1a; --muted:#6e6e6e; --accent:#08519C; --rule:#e6e6e6; --bg:#fbfbfc; }
body { margin:0; font-family:Arial,Helvetica,"IBM Plex Sans",sans-serif;
       color:var(--ink); background:var(--bg); line-height:1.6; }
.wrap { max-width:980px; margin:0 auto; padding:0 40px; }
.site-header { border-bottom:2px solid var(--rule); background:#fff; margin:0 -40px 0; }
.site-header .inner { max-width:980px; margin:0 auto; padding:38px 40px 28px; }
.site-header h1 { font-size:30px; font-weight:600; margin:0 0 6px; }
.site-header .sub { font-size:17px; color:#3a3a3a; margin:0 0 16px; }
.site-header .intro { font-size:15px; color:#333; max-width:760px; margin:0; }
.site-header .repo-cta { margin:14px 0 0; font-size:13.5px; }
.site-header .repo-cta a { color:var(--accent); font-weight:600; text-decoration:none; }
.site-header .repo-cta a:hover { text-decoration:underline; }
.sec-label { font-size:12px; letter-spacing:.08em; text-transform:uppercase;
             color:var(--muted); margin:26px 0 12px; font-weight:600; }
.list { display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:14px; }
.row { display:flex; gap:26px; background:#fff; border:1px solid var(--rule); border-radius:8px;
       padding:20px 24px; text-decoration:none; color:inherit;
       transition:box-shadow .15s, transform .15s, border-color .15s; }
.row.pub { border-left:4px solid var(--accent); position:relative; }
.row.pub:hover { box-shadow:0 6px 20px rgba(8,81,156,.10); transform:translateY(-1px); border-color:#cfd8e3; }
.row.planned { border-style:dashed; background:#fcfcfd; }
.row-id { flex:0 0 196px; }
.row-body { flex:1 1 auto; min-width:0; }
.badge { display:inline-block; min-width:54px; text-align:center; background:var(--accent);
         color:#fff; font-weight:700; font-size:13px; padding:5px 10px; border-radius:5px; }
.badge.muted { background:#c7ccd1; }
.dept-link { display:block; text-decoration:none; color:inherit; }
.dept-link::after { content:""; position:absolute; inset:0; }
.dept-link:hover .dept { color:var(--accent); }
.dept { font-size:16px; font-weight:600; line-height:1.3; margin:10px 0 0; }
.unit { font-size:12.5px; color:var(--muted); margin-top:3px; }
.blurb { font-size:14px; color:#333; margin:0 0 11px; line-height:1.55; }
.hl { margin:0 0 12px; padding-left:18px; }
.hl li { font-size:13px; color:#444; margin-bottom:4px; line-height:1.5; }
.ctas { display:flex; gap:20px; align-items:center; flex-wrap:wrap; }
.cta { font-size:13.5px; font-weight:600; color:var(--accent); }
.cta-slides { position:relative; z-index:1; font-size:13px; font-weight:600;
              color:var(--muted); text-decoration:none; }
.cta-slides:hover { color:var(--accent); }
.muted-txt { color:var(--muted); }
@media (max-width:640px) {
  .row { flex-direction:column; gap:14px; }
  .row-id { flex-basis:auto; }
  .dept { margin-top:8px; }
}
.site-footer { border-top:1px solid var(--rule); margin:26px -40px 0; background:#fff; }
.site-footer .inner { max-width:980px; margin:0 auto; padding:20px 40px 40px; }
.site-footer p { font-size:12.5px; color:var(--muted); margin:4px 0; }
.count { font-size:13px; color:var(--muted); text-transform:none; letter-spacing:0; }
```

```{r render, results="asis"}
esc <- function(s) {
  s <- as.character(s); s[is.na(s)] <- ""
  s <- gsub("&", "&amp;", s, fixed = TRUE)
  s <- gsub("<", "&lt;", s, fixed = TRUE)
  s <- gsub(">", "&gt;", s, fixed = TRUE)
  s
}

card <- function(a) {
  is_pub <- tolower(a[["status"]]) == "published"
  sl <- if (a[["short"]] %in% names(slides_paths)) slides_paths[[a[["short"]]]] else ""
  has_sl <- nzchar(sl)
  unit_line <- if (nzchar(a[["unit"]])) sprintf('<div class="unit">%s</div>', esc(a[["unit"]])) else ""
  slides_link <- if (has_sl) sprintf(
    '<a class="cta-slides" href="%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Briefing slides &#8599;</a>', esc(sl)) else ""

  if (is_pub) {
    dept <- sprintf('<a class="dept-link" href="%s"><div class="dept">%s</div></a>', esc(a[["output"]]), esc(a[["name"]]))
    rail <- sprintf('<div class="row-id"><span class="badge">%s</span>%s%s</div>', esc(a[["short"]]), dept, unit_line)
    hs <- trimws(unlist(strsplit(a[["highlights"]], "|", fixed = TRUE))); hs <- hs[nzchar(hs)]
    hl <- if (length(hs)) paste0('<ul class="hl">', paste0("<li>", esc(hs), "</li>", collapse = ""), '</ul>') else ""
    ctas <- sprintf('<div class="ctas"><span class="cta">Read the analysis &rarr;</span>%s</div>', slides_link)
    body <- sprintf('<div class="row-body"><p class="blurb">%s</p>%s%s</div>', esc(a[["blurb"]]), hl, ctas)
    sprintf('<div class="row pub">%s%s</div>', rail, body)
  } else {
    rail <- sprintf('<div class="row-id"><span class="badge muted">%s</span><div class="dept">%s</div>%s</div>',
                    esc(a[["short"]]), esc(a[["name"]]), unit_line)
    blurb <- if (has_sl) "Long-form report in preparation \u2014 briefing slides available now." else "Report in preparation."
    tail <- if (has_sl) sprintf('<div class="ctas">%s</div>', slides_link) else '<div class="cta muted-txt">Coming soon</div>'
    body <- sprintf('<div class="row-body"><p class="blurb muted-txt">%s</p>%s</div>', blurb, tail)
    sprintf('<div class="row planned">%s%s</div>', rail, body)
  }
}

cards <- apply(agencies, 1, card)
n_pub <- sum(tolower(agencies$status) == "published")
repo_link <- if (nzchar(repo_url)) sprintf('<a href="%s">Code &amp; data on GitHub</a> &middot; ', esc(repo_url)) else ""
repo_cta  <- if (nzchar(repo_url)) sprintf('<p class="repo-cta"><a href="%s">View the full code &amp; datasets on GitHub &rarr;</a></p>', esc(repo_url)) else ""

cat('<div class="wrap">')
cat(sprintf('<div class="site-header"><div class="inner"><h1>%s</h1><p class="sub">%s</p><p class="intro">%s</p>%s</div></div>',
            esc(site_title), esc(site_subtitle), esc(site_intro), repo_cta))
cat(sprintf('<div class="sec-label">Published reports &middot; <span class="count">%d available</span></div>', n_pub))
cat('<div class="list">', paste(cards, collapse = "\n"), '</div>', sep = "\n")
cat(sprintf('<div class="site-footer"><div class="inner"><p>%s</p><p>%sLast updated %s.</p></div></div>',
            esc(source_note), repo_link, format(Sys.Date(), "%B %Y")))
cat('</div>')
```
